summary: Gruber declares iA Writer his favorite iOS Markdown writing app, praising its beauty and focus-friendly design. He acknowledges Jason Snell's criticism about lack of extensibility but explains that on iPad he prioritizes focus over customization, unlike on Mac where scriptability is essential. He highlights iA Writer's Markdown syntax styling, custom typefaces, and elegant design as standout features. The post is a brief, warm appreciation of an app that Gruber considers uniquely classy among writing tools.
key_points: iA Writer is Gruber's top iOS Markdown editor despite its lack of extensibility, because iPad writing is about focus not customization
The app's Markdown syntax styling — outdented headings, real italic/bold rendering, great colors — sets the gold standard
iA Writer's custom typefaces (Mono, Duo, Quattro) are good enough to justify the presumptuous choice of excluding other fonts
Different platforms call for different tool philosophies: Mac demands scriptability, iPad rewards simplicity and beauty
money_quotes: The right words for each sentence, sentences that snap together into paragraphs, paragraphs ordered properly into sections, sections that together form a complete piece.
I just find iPadOS naturally better for focusing on a single task.
While I'd never consider using a Mac writing app that wasn't richly scriptable and customizable, it's not an issue for me on iPad.
To me it's the gold standard for Markdown syntax styling.
iA Writer is even so presumptuous as to only use its own custom typefaces: Mono, Duo, and Quattro. But they pull it off.
The best way I can put it is that iA Writer is a classy app.
BBEdit and MarsEdit are like pint glasses or coffee mugs. Occasionally, though, the mood calls for an elegant champagne flute. That's iA Writer for me.
key_insight: The best writing tools match the context of use — iPad rewards elegant simplicity and focus, making iA Writer's opinionated design choices virtues rather than limitations.
